name: Apricot (food medium)

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Fratianni et al. 2021 PMC8230439 (glucose ~22% of total sugars, 2.28-4.31 g/100g) scaled to USDA total sugars 9.24 g; USDA FDC 171697
components.evidence: Second most abundant sugar in ripe apricot flesh.

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: Fratianni et al. 2021 PMC8230439 (fructose ~15% of total sugars, 1.22-4.19 g/100g); USDA FDC 171697
components.evidence: Minor sugar fraction in ripe apricot.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Fratianni et al. 2021 PMC8230439 (sucrose >63% of total sugars, 4.15-10.13 g/100g); USDA FDC 171697 total sugars 9.24 g
components.evidence: Dominant sugar in ripe apricot (>60% of total sugars).

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Trace disaccharide.

components.name: D-sorbitol
components.source: Genus Prunus characteristic; not quantified in fetched primary sources
components.evidence: Sorbitol is a diagnostic translocated carbohydrate of Prunus fruit (typically 0.3-1 g/100g), present but not quantified here.

components.name: starch
components.evidence: Near-zero in ripe apricot (declines during ripening); present only in immature fruit.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Fratianni et al. 2021 PMC8230439 (citric ~55% of organic acids, 0.55-1.17 g/100g, midpoint)
components.evidence: Dominant organic acid in apricot; MISSING from prior FooDB profile.

components.name: L-malic acid
components.source: Fratianni et al. 2021 PMC8230439 (malic ~25% of organic acids, 0.40-1.43 g/100g, midpoint)
components.evidence: Co-dominant organic acid; already listed as L-Malate but was unquantified.

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Fratianni et al. 2021 PMC8230439 (succinic ~20% of organic acids, 0.33-0.56 g/100g, midpoint)
components.evidence: Third organic acid; MISSING from prior profile.

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Fratianni review (S2214799317301789) - detected in apricot, variably present
components.evidence: Present but not consistently quantified across cultivars.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.source: Fratianni review (S2214799317301789), 1.6-9.7 mg/g FW range across cultivars
components.evidence: Minor organic acid, highly cultivar-dependent.

components.name: shikimic acid
components.evidence: Detected in apricot; wide cultivar variability.

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Also functions as an organic acid.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Low protein fruit.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Most abundant free/bound amino acid in apricot.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Low-fat fruit flesh.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid of apricot flesh.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Consolidates duplicate FooDB iron entries (Fe2+, Fe3+, Iron).

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Principal cation of apricot.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Trace micronutrient, measured.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.source: Present in apricot; not quantified by USDA FDC 171697
components.evidence: Included as present-but-unquantified.

components.name: vitamin A (retinol activity equiv.)
components.evidence: From provitamin-A carotenoids (no preformed retinol in plant).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Principal apricot carotenoid / provitamin A.

components.name: water
components.evidence: Bulk matrix.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt ion is a FooDB trace/predicted entry with no measured value in apricot flesh; not a defined growth nutrient of this food.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Trace/contaminant element, not a measured nutrient of apricot; FooDB noise.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Duplicate of iron; consolidated into a single 'iron' entry (0.39 mg/100g).

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Compartment-tagged duplicate iron artifact from FooDB; consolidated into 'iron'.

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: Synthetic contraceptive estrogen; biologically implausible constituent of a fruit; FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Microbial fermentation preservative, not a native apricot constituent; FooDB noise.

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Stereochemical/diastereomer artifact of isoleucine; not a distinct real metabolite.

remove.name: Chinese tannin (Chinese gallotannin)
remove.reason: Gall-derived commercial tannin, not a native apricot compound; FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae/citrus, not measured in apricot flesh; FooDB predicted/implausible.

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: Predicted flavor/aroma trace, not a growth-relevant nutrient.

missing_added: citric acid (dominant organic acid, absent from prior profile)
quinic acid, fumaric acid, shikimic acid (minor organic acids)
quantitative amounts for L-malate/malic acid (previously unquantified)
individual sugar amounts (sucrose dominant, then glucose, then fructose)
D-sorbitol (Prunus-characteristic sugar alcohol, present but unquantified)
full USDA amino-acid, fatty-acid, mineral and vitamin amounts
keep_uncovered: chlorogenic acid / neochlorogenic acid (3-CQA, major apricot phenolic)
procyanidin B1, B3, B7 and trimers
caffeic acid, p-coumaric acid, ferulic acid, 3-/5-feruloylquinic acid, 5-p-coumaroylquinic acid
amygdalin (genuine cyanogenic glycoside of Prunus, mainly kernel/seed)
boron (genuine plant micronutrient, no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 171697 (SR Legacy, 'Apricots, raw') — macros, amino acids, fatty acids, minerals, vitamins, carotenoids
Fratianni et al. 2021, 'Nutritional and Phytochemical Traits of Apricots (Prunus armeniaca L.)', PMC8230439 — sugar (sucrose/glucose/fructose) and organic acid (citric/malic/succinic) quantification
Fratianni et al. 'Apricots: biochemistry and functional properties', ScienceDirect S2214799317301789 — quinic/fumaric/shikimic acid presence and ranges
FooDB FOOD00144 (source of prior component list; noise flagged)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for microbial growth are sucrose (~5.8 g/100g) plus its hexoses glucose (~2.0) and fructose (~1.4), and the organic acids — citric (~0.86) and malic (~0.90 g/100g) are co-dominant with succinic (~0.44); citric and succinic were entirely absent from the prior FooDB profile. Apricot is a high-potassium (259 mg/100g), beta-carotene-rich (1094 mcg/100g), low-protein/low-fat fruit.
